definition: Financial conditions indices are systematically distorted by AI-driven equity concentration. Cap-weighted vs. equal-weighted S&P 500 divergence of 1000-1500bp cumulative (2024-H1 2026), widest since 1999-2000. Top 10 firms represent ~35% of index weight but only ~3-4% of US employment, so the FCI equity component captures conditions of firms employing ~3-4% of workforce extrapolated to the economy. Creates 'cliff risk' where aggregate data stays resilient until median-firm stress cascades.
evidence: Cap-weighted vs. equal-weighted S&P 500 divergence of 1000-1500bp cumulative
Top 10 firms: ~35% of index weight but ~3-4% of US employment
NFIB small business optimism and regional Fed surveys show more stress than aggregate data
open_questions: Can the 75-100bp FCI distortion be measured with sufficient precision to be actionable?
When does the median-firm stress become visible in aggregate data?
Is the Fed aware of and adjusting for this FCI distortion?
